items.Phrase: If all units from both players are destroyed before the end of combat, who wins?
items.Main: In the case of both sides losing all their units and bastions in combat, combat immediately ends as there is no one left to fight the battle. If this happens before assign damage step do not resolve the rest of the resolution round, nor any other steps of combat. If the defender has a structure in that Area, then he keeps control of the structure and the planet.

items.Phrase: Many scheme cards read: 'Instead of revealing an order during the Operations Phase...' (ex. Mob Up!) - Can a player use such scheme card when he has no order tokens on the game board or if he does not have an order token that is on top of a stack?
items.Main: A player must have the option to reveal an order (having one on the board and it being at the top of a stack) to use any “Instead of revealing an order during the Operations phase…” ability.

items.Phrase: Are reinforcement tokens limited to 3 even in combat?
items.Main: The intent is that each player can only have 3 of each type of token in their play Area at a time. If a player has 3 tokens in their play Area, they can still play a card that adds a token to the battle from the supply. If there are no tokens in the supply though, then the effect of the card is wasted as no more tokens can be gained.

items.Phrase: Do Reinforcement Tokens count as a specific unit for Battle Card Effect?
items.Main: Yes. They are level 0 units and are treated the same as a plastic mini for all effect purposes during the Execution step of the combat. They can be retreated or stay until the end of combat, provide morale in Resolution step of combat and are returned to supply in the cleanup phase.

items.Phrase: Can I destroy a routed unit to execute a card effect?
items.Main: If a card effect doesn't specify routed or unrouted units, then yes you can choose that unit.

items.Phrase: When I gain/move a unit (in combat) due to a Battle Card effect, do I gain/lose its dice?
items.Main: No, unless the card specifically states so.

items.Phrase: Are there differences between vertical and horizontal warp storms?
items.Main: No. There is no difference between warp storms. Rotate the event card and move the warp storm appropriately.

items.Phrase: If you draw multiple Event cards, do you have to show all of them?
items.Main: No. Just reveal the one you will play.

items.Phrase: Can I use multiple Order Upgrade Cards on the same order?
items.Main: Yes. Multiple Order Upgrade Cards can be used during the same order.

items.Phrase: Are Areas in different system, but sharing a gold border, still considered Adjacent?
items.Main: Yes. But be careful, since some card effects also specify 'Active System'.

items.Phrase: Can I place an order I can't resolve (e.g. Strategize in a system without my units) just to block?
items.Main: Yes. But order other placement restrictions still apply (like friendly Areas in an adjacent system).

items.Phrase: Do I have to use Upgraded Orders if I resolve an order?
items.Main: No. An Upgraded Order can be used once per round, and you would have to declare at the appropriate moment (stated on the Upgrade Order) that this order would be an Enhanced one.

items.Phrase: If I choose not to resolve the order – do I have to show it?
items.Main: Yes. You always have to reveal the order before you choose to resolve it or not.

items.Phrase: Can I attack with more units than the Areas capacity?
items.Main: Yes. Unit capacity limits are checked at the end of the Order.  Advance order only restricts you to  a maximum of 5 units per movement restrictions to a single Area, and to 8 dice per combat.

items.Phrase: Does a player get eliminated the moment his last Friendly Area is contested?
items.Main: No. Battle finishes, and then you determine if the player is eliminated.

items.Phrase: Can I move the same units twice with two Advance orders?
items.Main: Yes, as long as those units are not routed. Unit can participate in more than one battle per round.

items.Phrase: Do ships in adjacent voids contribute to ground combat?
items.Main: No. An advance action can create only one contested Area, and combat (void or ground) is fought between the units in that single Area. Orbital strikes allow ships to attack ground units in adjacent Areas, provided no other contested Area was formed by the Advance order.

items.Phrase: Can units move 'through' an Uncontrolled world in a single advance action, provided a different unit stays there to make it friendly?
items.Main: No. Ground movement requires tracing a legal path at the BEGINNING of the ground movement stage. That means you must stop at Uncontrolled Worlds if you can't skip past them via a friendly Area.

items.Phrase: Can I trace a path for a Unit to move through more than 2 systems via legal path (to go around Warp or to avoid combat)?
items.Main: No. You can only trace a path through the unit’s current system, and the Active System. So maximum 2 systems.

items.Phrase: Can I trace a path for a Unit to retreat through more than 2 systems via legal path (to go around Warp)?
items.Main: No. Retreat is a type of move and can be performed either in the active system or to an adjacent system with a legal path to it, that was not the system the attacker came from.

items.Phrase: Can I move ground units from system to system without a ship?
items.Main: Yes. When planets are adjacent, you can move between them regardless of what System they are in.

items.Phrase: When I want to move my units, and I choose to move to an adjacent system, do *all* units that move have to move to that system, or can I move units inside the adjacent system (so they will come “closer” to the Active System)?
items.Main: No. Partial moves are not allowed. All movement must end up in the Activated System.

items.Phrase: Can I move a ship into battle, but then move ground units through the same ship onto Friendly or Uncontrolled Worlds?
items.Main: No. Your ship has created a contested Area, and thus your ground units cannot trace a legal path through a Friendly Areas before resolving the combat.

items.Phrase: How many units can I move in an Advance Order?
items.Main: You can move unlimited units, but only 5 can end up in a Contested Area. They all have to have a legal path at the beginning of the move, and you can only create 1 Contested Area. At the end of the move all units that exceed the area’s capacity will be destroyed.

items.Phrase: When moving ships into the System, do they all go into one Void?
items.Main: No. Each ship chooses their own void as long as that doesn't create more than 1 contested Area.

items.Phrase: Can I voluntarily destroy units in order to deploy them again?
items.Main:
In a way. You cannot remove existing figures. However, you may destroy them at the end of a Deploy Order if you exceed unit capacity in their Area. Keep in mind new units have to be placed to achieve that.
An Advance Order could achieve the same result if you move enough units to the same Area and exceed unit capacity.

items.Phrase: Can I deploy units to an area already at unit capacity?
items.Main: Yes. However, you need to destroy units that exceed unit capacity in that area.

items.Phrase: Can I voluntarily remove Structures so I can build a different structure on that world?
items.Main: No. Someone else may destroy them though effects, but unless you have card with effect that say otherwise, once you build yourself a structure, that world can't be built on again.

items.Phrase: Can I voluntarily destroy structures on a Friendly World?
items.Main: No. Someone else may destroy them though effects, but unless you have card with effect that say otherwise, once you build yourself a structure, that world can't be built on again.

items.Phrase: Can I deploy ships into an Enemy Void and start a combat?
items.Main:
No. You may only deploy to Uncontrolled or Friendly Areas in the Active System.
This is a problem for non-Eldar factions as they only have one void in their home tile. An enemy can prevent you from purchasing ships by taking that void assuming that you have only 1 factory in the home tile.

items.Phrase: Can I use multiple forge tokens for a unit purchase?
items.Main:
More than 1 forge token can be used in only in this scenario:
- First one is used to lower the unit’s Command Level requirement by 1
- Second one is used to meet the forge token requirement (e.g. Titan requirement)

items.Phrase: Can you use a cache token to reduce the cost of a purchase to 0?
items.Main: Yes. Keeping in mind that only one cache token can be used per deployed unit.

items.Phrase: If I have two factories in a system, is the max units I can purchase based on the factory with the highest unit capacity?
items.Main: No. The deploy limit is the combined unit capacity from all Friendly Worlds with a Factory in the Active System.

items.Phrase: Do I still get to resolve my Dominate Faction bonus if I choose to put the Order Token on top of the Event Deck instead of resolving normally?
items.Main: No. By placing the Order Token on top of your Event deck, you chose not to resolve Dominate.

items.Phrase: Do I still get assets from friendly worlds if I choose to put the Order Token on top of the Event Deck instead of resolving normally?
items.Main: No. By placing the Order Token on top of your Event deck, you chose not to resolve Dominate.

items.Phrase: Are the results of Strategize public information?
items.Main: Yes. You show face up what cards were purchased, and what were removed.

items.Phrase: Do planets have to be adjacent to my ships in order to orbital strike?
items.Main: Yes. And in the same (active) system.  That means the corner planet in a 3 world system cannot be normally targeted by Orbital Strike.

items.Phrase: Does damage during Orbital Strike apply against all units?
items.Main: No. Damage is applied the same as in combat. If you rolled 4 }, your opponent takes 4 damage. Your opponent has to assign that damage, and destroy units appropriately. If the damage was not equal or greater than units HP that unit is not routed.

items.Phrase: Are Orbital Strikes limited to 8 dice?
items.Main: Yes

items.Phrase: Can I use Orbital Strike a planet with just a structure?
items.Main: Yes. You can always choose to use Orbital Strike on any Enemy World, and use any Order Upgrade Cards you want to. If the structure is a City/Factory, since they have no HP, they cannot be destroyed.

items.Phrase: Can I take over planets by destroying all ground units using Orbital Strikes?
items.Main: No. Structures retain their affiliation even if all ground units are destroyed by the orbital strike.

items.Phrase: What happens when a Bastion is assigned damage?
items.Main: Bastions take damage like a unit, but cannot be routed like one. That means they are always available to assign damage. If you assign 3 or more damage, they will be destroyed though. This implies that they can soak up to 2 damage each round. Since you must assign all damage to a single target, before moving to the next one, you will frequently be assigning damage to the Bastion last so that it will remain. Watch out for enemy morale though since it is an easy way to give away your structure to your opponent.

items.Phrase: Can a person with a Bastion rout it for any card effects (e.g. Lure of Chaos)?
items.Main: No. Bastions cannot be routed by any effect.
If you win a combat by Morale, then you take over the Bastion.

items.Phrase: Can Bastion fight in combat without any other units?
items.Main: Yes. It provides 2 dice, and still activate some cards.

items.Phrase: Can Bastion fight when they are on the same world only with routed units?
items.Main: Yes. This causes routed units not to retreat. Bastion still provides 2 dice and routed do not provide any dice.

items.Phrase: Does a bastion count as a unit for reinforcement token placement purposes?
items.Main: No. You are allowed to use 1 token per unit, and Bastions are not units.

items.Phrase: Can I add reinforcement tokens if in combat there is a Bastion with just routed units?
items.Main: Yes. Rules do not require for the units to be unrouted to count to the maximum tokens that can be placed into the combat.

items.Phrase: Can I retreat if I moved via Combat Card or Order Upgrade Effects?
items.Main: If none of the units in the battle moved to the contested Area as part of the Advance order (meaning they moved to that Area as part of the movement granted by the Advance order), then they cannot retreat after or mid-battle. That also means they cannot retreat using retreat abilities from players' or opponents' combat cards. Only “move” cards allow you to get the unit out of the combat. This means that units that start combat with cards like Wave Serpent, Orc Rocks, Drop Pods, Mycetic Spores, or Revel in Bloodshed cannot retreat if they lose combat, and therefore are destroyed.

items.Phrase: As an attacker, do I have to always retreat back to where my units came from?
items.Main: If any units moved into the contested Area via an Advance Order, all attacking units must move to a single Area that at least one unit moved from to start the combat. If all units were placed in the contested Area by any effect other than an Advance Order, all attacking units cannot retreat and are destroyed. Note that it is irrelevant if an Area is Friendly or Uncontrolled at the time of the attacker’s retreat.

items.Phrase: When defending, can I choose to retreat to an Uncontrolled Area if there is a legal path to a Friendly Area?
items.Main: The defending player always has to retreat to the friendly Area if a legal path exists, and then destroy units down to the unit capacity of that Area. If there is no legal path to a Friendly Area, but a legal path exists to an Uncontrolled Area, then retreating units move to the Uncontrolled Area.

items.Phrase: When defending, can I choose to retreat to an Uncontrolled Area if there is a Friendly Area in legal path, but it is at maximum unit capacity?
items.Main: The defending player always has to retreat to the friendly Area if a legal path exists, and then destroy units down to the unit capacity of that Area. If there is no legal path to a Friendly Area, but a legal path exists to an Uncontrolled Area, then retreating units move to the Uncontrolled Area.

items.Phrase: Can you move a routed unit via Order or Card Effect?
items.Main: No. The rules explicitly prevent Move and Place for a routed unit. Routed units can only retreat. The only exception to that rule is Farsight due to the unique phrase “all units”.

items.Phrase: Do routed units automatically retreat when attacked?
items.Main: If you attack a planet with ONLY routed defenders and no structures, they all will automatically retreat if they can meet the rules for retreat, else they are destroyed. No battle occurs.

items.Phrase: If I only have routed units, and it takes some additional damage, does it get destroyed?
items.Main: Not necessarily. Routed units must still take damage equal or greater than HP in order to be destroyed.

items.Phrase: Can Ground Units move through Routed Ships?
items.Main: Yes. Routed ships still create a Friendly Area.
Yes. Routed or not, it is irrelevant for the purpose of objective collection.

items.Phrase: If I win combat with routed units, do my units rally immediately?
items.Main: No. Wait until the end of the round.

items.Phrase: Can unit with 1 hit point become routed?
items.Main: Yes. Even if they only have 1 damage, they can be routed through card effects or retreats.

items.Phrase: Does in a 2 player game the 3rd tile placed by each player has 0 objectives on it?
items.Main: Yes. You must fill objective spots with unique objectives when you place a tile, and since there’s only 1 unique opponent’s objective per tile and you only have 2 objectives to place in a 2p game, your last tile will be empty of objectives.

items.Phrase: Does in a 3 player game the 3rd tile placed by each player has 0 objectives on it?
items.Main: Yes. You must fill all objective spots when you place a tile, and since there’s 2 per tile and you only have 4 objectives to place in a 3p game, your last tile will be empty of objectives.

items.Phrase: Do I control Areas in my Faction Tile without units/structures on them?
items.Main: No. There is nothing special about Faction Tiles, other than it’s an ensured setup option with lots of unit capacity and assets. You always need an unit or a structure to make an Area Friendly.

items.Phrase: Do I have to place my Faction tile first?
items.Main: No. You can choose any other tile as first.

items.Phrase: Can my opponents lock me in a corner with their warp storm placements?
items.Main: Yes. The only limitation is that two warp storms cannot be stacked.

Q: Does damage from both 'asset damage steps' stack?
A: No. Damage does not stack, it is two separate instances of damage.

Q: If there is a unit with 3 hit points in combat and enemy is dealing it 2 damage. What happens when Armoured Advance’s ability is resolved? Is the unit destroyed?
A: The unit would take 2 instances of 2 damage. If unrouted, the first instance of damage would rout it. The second instance of damage would do nothing since it’s not equal or greater than that units HP.

Q: Does the resolve additional assess damage step count for both sides or just for your opponent?
A: Both sides resolve assess damage step twice. Keep in mind the effects without 'must' are optional.

Q: Does combat tokens are used in both assess damage steps?
A: Yes. They are not discarded until the end of the second assess damage step.

Q: Can I target the chosen card in the defender’s hand?
A: No. Break the Line only applies to face up cards. Playing this card as an attacker for your first card would 'waste' the effect since no face up cards are present at that moment.

Q: Can this cancel battle-long effects?
A: Yes. Break the Line may cancel on-going effects, like ex. Weird Boyz. Most effects are resolved immediately and cannot be undone afterwards.

Q: Do you get to roll dice when you move the Marine as a result of this card’s battle effect?
A: No. Dice provided by Units are only rolled at Preparation step of combat, before Execution rounds.

Q: Who chooses which die to lose?
A: Unless explicitly specified, the owner of the combat card makes the choice.

Q: If I'm Defending, do I still get attack/defense tokens?
A: Yes. Each paragraph is treated separate, so you can pick your two tokens.

That response is based on the FFG stance, with mindset “rules as written”

Q: Can my opponent choose to rout a routed unit?
A: No. The rules state you cannot rout a unit that is already routed.

Q: If my opponent has no units to rout, do I gain a die?
A: Yes.

Q: If my opponent only has a bastion, do I gain a die?
A: Yes.

Q: Do I have to say what die I would choose before my opponent chooses if he wants to rout a unit?
A: No. Your opponent must choose whether or not to rout one of his units before the Chaos player chooses which die he gains from this card’s general ability.

Q: Can my opponent choose to rout a routed unit?
A: No. However, if all of his units are already routed, then there is no further effect.

Those responses are based on the FFG stance, with mindset “rules as written”

Q: Can my opponent choose to rout a routed unit to gain the die?
A: No. The rules state you cannot rout a unit that is already routed.

Q: If my opponent has no units to rout, do I gain free Cultist/Iconoclast?
A: Yes.

Q: If my opponent only has a bastion, do I gain free Cultist/Iconoclast?
A: Yes.

Q: Does the cultist get added to battle or is just placed on the world?
A: The new cultist enters combat, but it does not roll additional dice.

Q: If my opponent already has 8 dice, can he still rout a unit to prevent me from gaining a free unit?
A: Yes. The “does not” refers only to the “choose and rout 1 of his units”.

That response is based on last page of the manual

Q: What happens if my opponnet has no routed unit to destroy? 
A: Chaos automatically gains 2 @.

Q: Can I replace a routed cultist with a Chaos Space Marine?
A: Yes. Also the newly placed Chaos Space Marine is placed as unrouted.

Q: Can I replace a reinforcement token with a Chaos Space Marine?
A: Yes. During combat reinforcement tokens are treated as Command Level 0 units for all purposes.

Q: Do I get a card from my opponent’s hand?
A: No, you get a card from the remaining cards from the combat deck.

Q: If play them two of them, do I get 2x the tokens?
A: Yes. Those are two on-going effects that stack.

Q: Can I orbital strike twice using this upgrade?
A: No. An orbital strike is not a combat, so the enhanced order cannot be used.

Q: Can I move to a planet diagonally?
A: No. It states Adjacent Area, which means they must share a border.

Q: If I move my last unit, do I still do damage?
A: Yes, the assess damage phase still occurs, after which you would jump to the Resolution Phase.

Q: If I move my last unit, does that unit still get attacked in the execution round?
A: No. The unit is no longer in the battle, so even though the execution round finishes and assess damage step is performed, the retreated unit is not targetable for damage.

Q: Can my opponent choose to allow you to discard face up card if he has none(Psychic Lance played 1st round as attacker trigger that case)?
A: Yes. Due to this Eldar would not gain 4 #.

Q: Does this apply just to the Eldar units?
A: ALL units, including your opponent’s units, are immune to damage.

Q: Will a Bastion also be prevented from suffering damage?
A: No. Bastions are not a unit for the purposes of resolving battle effects.

Q: Can I move any Command Level 0 and 1 units anywhere on the board?
A: No, just to an adjacent area to the combat area. Since this is a move, this cannot include moving routed units, but can start combat as specified on the card.

Q: Can I use Wave Serpent a second time and create a second contested area?
A: No. The Eldar player can only move units into one contested area, regardless of how many Wave Serpent cards he plays during the combat.

Q: Can I use Wave Serpent a second time and move to a different location than the first one?
A: Yes.

Q: Does this mean I can have more than 1 structure per planet?
A: Yes. If you pay 2 materiel, you can add a Bastion to a world with a City or Factory.

Q: Do I have to wait until the next Turn to use this Chaos Event?
A: No. Schemes are available from the point you gain them. If you are 1st player during Events and Warp Storms step of Refresh Phase, you can interrupt the warp storm move of every other player.

Q: Since this is an Event, who gets to choose which unit is routed?
A: Unless explicitly specified, the owner of the event card makes the choice.

Q: Can I use it in other players combat?
A: No. Unless explicitly specified, Cards can only be used during your own combats.

Q: Does the Chaos player gains morale when he does not move through Warp Storm?
A: Yes.

Q: Can I get a combat upgrade with this?
A: Yes. When it says Upgrade, it means Order or Combat.

Q: If I had more events cards to choose from and I picked to play Incantation of Tzeentch, do I have to shuffle back remaining event cards before resolving Incantation?
A: No. Draw the 3 additional cards from Incantation of Tzeentch, resolve 1, and then shuffle all cards back (both, those from order tokens, and those from Tzeentch).

Q: Can I resolve warp movement from the drawn card?
A: No. Warp movements are prior to resolving the Event cards.

Q: Does the Ork player has to participate in combat to use this card?
A: Yes. Unless explicitly stated you can only use abilities within your own combat.

Q: Can I use it in other players combat?
A: No. Unless explicitly specified, cards can only be used during combats you participate in (as the attacker or the defender).

Q: Can I start a combat with this?
A: No, a player cannot start combat with this card. Correct way to phrase the card is “…take any of your units from 1 area and place them on any 1 friendly or uncontrolled area.”

Q: Do I get it to keep the reinforcement token or do I have to use it in the combat?
A: You gain the token into your play area, and therefore you don't have to use it immediately.

Q: Can you bombard an Uncontrolled World in hope to gain a free Space Marine?
A: No. You can only orbital strike an enemy world.

Q: Can I orbital strike an enemy world with only a structure to gain a free marine?
A: Yes. You can orbital strike any enemy world. Keep in mind that city and factory has no HP and cannot be destroyed, but they will be taken over by the Space Marine. If used on a Bastion this will create a contested Area and normal combat will commence if remaining dice from the Orbital Strike did not destroy it.

Q: Is there a limit to the number of marines I get from an Orbital Strike?
A: Yes. You are allowed to spend exactly 2 { to place 1 Space Marine.

Q: Do I need to meet command level requirement to upgrade my Scout/Marine?
A: No. This is a replace, not a purchase. Only purchases need to meet the command requirements

Q: Can I use a cache token to upgrade my Scout/Marine?
A: No. This is a replace, not a purchase. You can only use cache tokens for purchases.

Q: Do you need at least 1 factory to make this work?
A: No. All bastions in the Active System acts like a factory during that Deploy Order.

Q: Who chooses what is destroyed?
A: Your opponent is forced to choose what unit or structure is destroyed.

Q: Do Warp Storms prevent them from being placed in an adjacent system?
A: No. Warp storms only prohibit movement of units and do not remove adjacency.

Q: Do I need a factory in the active system?
A: No. The unit is purchased outside Deploy Order restrictions because the purchase resolves after that order, and you just need a friendly area to place the unit.

Q: Do I need a friendly unit or structure in the active system?
A: Yes. At least 1 friendly area is required.

Q: Do I need to meet the command requirement?
A: Yes. Unit purchases always have to respect the command level limit and materiel expenditure (in this case, discounted by the number of cultists).

Q: Can I purchase a ship?
A: Yes, but it has to go into a friendly void.

Q: Can I purchase Reaver Titan by using a forge token to reduce its command requirement to 2?
A: No. Forge tokens are spent to reduce a unit’s command requirement when purchasing, but they do not actually reduce the command level of the unit. For this reason you cannot use Forge tokens with Dread Ritual to purchase a command level 3 unit. A forge token can still be used to buy Hellbrute/Repulsive Grand Cruisers when you are currently on command Level 1.

Q: When purchasing this Order Upgrade, does it apply to the current Strategize order?
A: No, since the Strategize Order token is already revealed. It can only apply to a subsequent Strategize Orders.

Q: Can I move ground units?
A: No. It is explicitly stated that this apply only to ships.

Q: Do I still get my event if I decide the Strategize order token is something else?
A: No. Use of Green Tide means that order token is treated as something else, and only Strategize order tokens go on top of your Event deck after being resolved.

Q: Can I bridge an empty void to another ship, and then a planet?
A: Yes.

Q: Can they be used to retreat?
A: In a way. As a defender Ork Rocks cannot be used, since your advance order is not being resolved, and as an attacker you have to retreat to place you came from. Ork rocks can be only used once per turn, and if they were used to attack, then it cannot be used if the attack failed. If they were not used to attack, they are not useful to retreat, since attacker has special rules for retreating.

Q: Can you use cache/forge tokens?
A: Yes. Even ones you just got from the Dominate action.

Q: Do you need a factory to build?
A: No. Factory is only required when purchasing units during Deploy Order.

Q: Do you need to meet command level requirement?
A: Yes. Unit purchase always requires command level limit and materiel expenditure.

Q: Can I build a spaceship?
A: No. It explicitly says on planet, so ground units only.

Q: Can I purchase combat upgrade with this?
A: Yes. Wording on the card is off, since all orders can be “resolved”, but without any effect. The intent here is that you can fully resolve the order without unit requirement.

Q: If I had 1 ship and decided to move it to an adjacent system – do I resolve remaining dice?
A: Yes. The Eldar player resolves the effects of all dice rolled, even if he moves ships out of the system.

Q: Can I have a second structure of the same type?
A: No. Second structure has to be of a different type, than the one already on the world.

Q: If someone else conquers my planet with two structures, do they keep both?
A: Yes. Attackers gain control of all enemy structures on the world.

Q: Do I need a path to trigger this?
A: No. Place action does not need a legal path. Take a unit in the active system and place on a friendly world.

Q: Can my opponent still choose to not allow me to gain 2 (S), if the unit ability is resolved in the last execution round of a combat?
A: Yes. Your opponent’s choice is not limited in that case and he can choose either option. But not allowing you to gain 2 @ will not result of adding 2 to your defense value, since that was last round.

Q: Do I have to reroll 2 } or 2 <? 
A: No. You can mix types.

Q: If I rerolled my only }, do I still have to reroll a <?
A: Yes. If you have dice available to reroll, you must perform 2 rerolls.

Q: Is one ground unit type enough to gain 1 combat token? 
A: Yes, it’s per unit of different command level.

Q: Is one ship type enough to gain 2 combat tokens? 
A: Yes, it’s per unit of different command level.

Q: Can I reroll my opponent’s { with the secondary ability?
A: No. Unless explicitly specified, the owner can only use the ability on yourself.

Q: Is one ground unit type enough to gain 2 combat tokens? 
A: Yes, it’s per unit of different command level.

Q: Is one ship type enough to gain 3 combat tokens? 
A: Yes, it’s per unit of different command level.

Q: Is one unit type enough to gain 1 |​? 
A: Yes, it’s per unit of different command level.

Q: When I resolve this card’s ability, do I immediately decide which tokens my opponent loses?
A: No, you decide that at the start of the assess damage step.

Q: Do I have to reroll 2 { or 2 <? 
A: No. You can mix types.

Q: If I rerolled 1 {, do I still have to reroll 1 <?
A: Yes. If you have dice available to reroll, you must perform 2 rerolls.

Q: Is one unit type enough for your opponent to reroll 1 }? 
A: Yes, it’s per unit of different command level.

Q: When resolving the general ability, if I have fewer than 2 {, can I choose to reroll 2 { or am I forced to retreat a unit?
A: By the general ability you are forced to choose. The choice is to to either reroll 2 { or to retreat 1 of your units. You are obliged to resolve the general ability always when playing this card (because of the term must), but you can always choose either of the two options, even if you do not fulfill everything that is required. In case you have no {, do not reroll any dice; if you have 1 {, reroll just that one.

Q: What if my units cannot legally retreat for whatever reason?
A: If you still choose to retreat – the unit is destroyed.

Q: Is only 1 of all destroyed units can be retreated?
A: Yes.

Q: Does the damage in 2nd assess damage step, if Remorseless Advance/Armoured Advance was used, gets lost? 
A: Yes. Even though tokens are not discarded, if first assess damage step didn't destroy it, second will also not destroy it. If it did destroy it, a new unit cannot be chosen and the excess damage is lost.

Q: Can I spend a dice if I have no unit to rally (for example to lose guns if I want to win battle with bastion on morale)?
A: Yes. You are allowed to spend any number of dice. Rally is a benefit of that spending, but it’s not obligatory.

That response is based on the FFG stance, with mindset “rules as written”

Q: Can my opponent choose to rout a routed unit?
A: No. The rules state you cannot rout a unit that is already routed.

Q: If your opponent has no units to rout, does he lose a die?
A: Yes.

Q: If my opponent only has a bastion, do he lose a die?
A: Yes.

Q: Can i use this ability on my reinforcement tokens?
A: Yes. During combat reinforcement tokens are treated as Command Level 0 units for all purposes.

Q: Does second ability interfere anyhow with first ability? 
A: No, taking damage and routing are different. You can take damage and not rout.

Q: Can I only use this card when I am the attacker?
A: Yes.

Q: When can I play the cards to use it?
A: Before you roll dice or draw cards. It’s explicitly states “start of combat”.

Q: Does Shadow in The Warp prevent my opponent from placing a reinforcement token in the contested area from the supply?
A: Yes. Only exception to this is Lead the Rightous, since the token is already used prior to the combat.

Q: When resolving this event, if I choose to play a combat card from my upgrade deck, what happens to that card at the end of the combat?
A: A card that was played from the upgrade deck while resolving Tactical Genius is returned to the upgrade deck at the end of the combat.

Q: When exactly this card needs to be used?
A: When you would play a combat card, you may discard it to play a combat card from either your combat deck or your combat upgrade deck. Combat cards are played simultaneously, so your opponent does not have prior knowledge that you would use this scheme when choosing his combat card.

Q: Can I use the scheme in other players' combats?
A: No. Unless explicitly specified, Cards can only be used during your own combats.

Q: Can it destroy a Bastion that had damage assigned to it?
A: No. Bastions are not units.

Q: If a unit suffers damage but is not routed due to another card's effect, can this card be used to destroy it?
A: Yes.

Q: Can I use this card when an opponent's unit is routed by another card's effect?
A: No. It is explicitly specified the unit has to suffer damage. Orbital strike doesn't count either since it’s explicitly specified it has to be during combat.

Q:  Does Denial of Supplies prevent my opponent from placing reinforcement tokens from the supply in the contested area?
A: No. As specified, it only applies to reinforcement tokens placed from a player’s play area.

Q: Are the basic combat cards considered to have a command level of 0 for the purpose of this event?
A: No. Basic combat cards have no command level. When resolving Philosophy of War, treat your basic combat cards as having a command level lower than any combat upgrade cards.
See reference rulebook entries “Command Level” (2nd bullet point) and “Combat cards and combat upgrade cards” (5th bullet point) for further information.

Q: Does my opponent know what cards I'm replacing?
A: Yes. Content of your combat deck is always public information.

Q: Can I purchase this order upgrade card before purchasing the Breeding Chambers upgrade? 
A: Yes, but in order to use it you need to have both cards purchased.

Q: Does the free reinforcement token count against the normal limit to the number of reinforcement tokens I can use in the combat?
A: No, despite being placed in the area at the start of the reinforce step. See reference rulebook entry “Reinforcement Tokens” (last bullet point) for further information.

Q: When resolving Departmento Munitorum’s limited Advance Order, can I resolve an orbital strike?
A: Yes. Departmento Munitorum’s ability text states all of the applicable limitations of the limited orders. In case of the limited Advance Order you are only allowed to move units from 1 area, which is the only difference between Departmento Munitorum’s limited and a regular Advance order.

Q: Can Order Upgrade Cards can be used when resolving the limited order?
A: Yes.

Q:  Does the passive ability work even if Forced Recruitment was used?
A: Yes. The bottom ability is a permanent one.

Q: Can I use this order upgrade when resolving Mass Production (faction ability)? 
A: Yes.

Q: Can I always use the part of the faction ability that allows me reduce the cost of 2 Command Level 0 units by 1 each by spending a cache token or only just when resolving a faction ability?
A: Only when resolving your faction ability.

Q: Can I purchase structures when using the faction ability?
A: No, you are only allowed to purchase units.

Q: Can I resolve an orbital strike when resolving the first Advance Order and then start a combat with the second Advance Order?
A: Yes.

Q: When resolving the first Advance Order, the ability says that if combat occurs, only 1 execution round is resolved. What happens if both sides have units and/or bastions remaining in the area at the end of that execution round?
A: In that case the player with the highest morale value wins the combat, as per regular combat resolution rules.

Q: Can I use reinforcement as Tau in battle that lasts 1 round?
A: Yes. It is considered a normal combat, that only lasts 1 execution round instead of typical 3.

Q: Do I add the reinforcement token to the combat with the general ability?
A: No. You gain it in your play area, and can add it to the combat with the unit’s ability.

Q: If my opponent gained a |, and rolled morale, can he keep it?
A: Yes. The ability blocks gaining of < explicitly.

Q: Can I replace routed Sisters Repentia?
A: Yes. As long as there is a unroute Sisters Repentia you can replace the routed one.

Q: Can I replace a reinforcement token?
A: Yes. During combat reinforcement tokens are treated as Command Level 0 units for all purposes.

Q: Do I have to select { or < exclusively?
A: No. You don’t have to spend one die type exclusively. You can spend any mix of dice, and your opponent loses as much } as the sum of all dice spent by you.

Q: Does the effect stack?
A: Yes. If two cards were played, the health value of your units is increased by 2.

Q: Does the effect persist if the card was discarded?
A: No. The game has no memory, and therfore if the card was discarded the effect stopped.

Q: Can I firstly use Orbital Strike and then create a Contested Area, and start a combat?
A: No. The rule that you cannot start an Orbital Strike when there already was a combat still applies to the whole round. Please choose one.

Q: Can I move the Living Saint unit diagonally?
A: No. Resolve a move trough a legal path.

Q: What is this ability of this event card?
A: This card allows moving the warp storm in any direction and does not have additional ability.

Q: Can I use this card if I didn’t strategize this turn?
A: Yes.

Q: Is combined morale sum of all unrouted units morale value?
A: Partially. The defender also adds morale value of bastions he owns on that world.

Q: Do I add morale value of all of my ships in area of the Strike?
A: No. Just the sum of all unrouted ships.

Q: Can I add second reinforcement token to the world with the bastion if there is already one there?
A: No. It’s explicitly specified it has to be a world without a reinforcement token.

Q: If I have a bastion with a reinforcement token, can I add a second reinforcement token from my play area (since reinforcement token counts as a unit)?
A: No. It’s explicitly specified in the rules that maximum number of reinforcement tokens a player can use during a combat is equal to the number of plastic units he has in that combat.

Q: If I have a bastion with a reinforcement token, can I roll dice for the reinforcement token (since reinforcement token counts as a unit during combat)?
A: Sisters Repentia provide 0 dice, so it makes no difference.
